summary.intent: Implement a framework-agnostic DropZone core component (issue #218) with state machine, validation, callbacks, ConnectApi attributes, and snapshots, then get it through Codex automated code review.
summary.outcome: PR #674 was opened, Codex review completed with four findings, all were addressed (separate timed-reset event, file-only max_files counting, mid-drag disabled/read-only normalization, stale timer cancellation on re-entry), and the remediation was committed and pushed with 96.45% line coverage.

repo: The project uses a Codex automated reviewer triggered via `@codex review` PR comment; it signals active review with 👀 reaction and posts inline threads when done.
The review workflow is defined in `.agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md` and `/Users/ericson/.claude/skills/receiving-code-review/SKILL.md`.
Proptest regression seeds are persisted in `crates/ars-components/proptest-regressions/state-machines.txt` and must be committed when new shrunken cases are found.
Spec files live in three parallel locations: `spec/components/`, `spec/leptos-components/`, and `spec/dioxus-components/` and must be kept in sync.

The full verification suite for a component includes: `cargo xci`, unit tests, spec conformance tests, proptest (with `--ignored`), insta snapshot tests, `cargo xtask spec validate`, clippy, llvm-cov, and cargo mutants.
PR labels include `snapshot-reviewed` which must be applied manually after snapshot review passes.

code.finding: The timed visual reset and explicit form reset were previously handled by the same event; Codex flagged this as a state-machine edge case requiring separation into distinct events (AutoReset vs Reset).
max_files validation was counting all dragged items; it should count only file-type items, not directory entries or other item kinds.
Props changes (disabled/read-only) occurring mid-drag must force an exit from the drag state rather than silently ignoring the prop update.

A stale proptest invariant assumed form_data() is only available in DropAccepted state; after adding AutoReset, the machine can be Idle while accepted form_data remains accessible by design — the invariant needed to track accepted payload presence rather than state.

workflow: The Codex review polling pattern uses GitHub reactions API to detect 👀 (active) state and GraphQL reviewThreads to detect when findings land; polling every 120s is the established cadence.
When remediating review findings: add regression tests first (to confirm the fix is needed and tested), then patch the state machine, then update specs, then run the full validation suite.
Co-author trailers should include the reviewing agent: `Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>` for Codex-reviewed commits.
Coverage target for touched modules is tracked per-run; 96%+ line coverage is acceptable for this codebase.

friction: Codex review took 8+ minutes with multiple 120s polling cycles before findings appeared, requiring 6+ sleep-poll iterations.
New regression tests caused compile failures before the state machine was updated to add the separate AutoReset event, requiring careful sequencing of test-first then implementation changes.
The proptest suite found a stale invariant only after all other tests passed, requiring an additional investigation and patch cycle.
open_items: CI had coverage/feature-flag/release jobs still pending when the session ended — these were not confirmed green.
Codex inline threads were not explicitly resolved in the transcript after the remediation push; re-triggering review and confirming approval was not shown as completed.
cargo mutants run (`-f crates/ars-components/src/utility/drop_zone/mod.rs`) was listed in the verification matrix but not shown as executed in the remediation phase.
